entry.title: 3F0 supports settlement accounts from SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2502
entry.content:
Introduction

For Event-Based Production Cost Posting (3F0), in previous releases, cost distribution for non-material receiver categories, such as cost center, WBS element, and order, can only be done with the original goods issue, activity confirmation, and overhead accounts.

As of this release, you can choose By Settlement Account as the non-material receiver account determination method and assign a specific G/L account for the cost distribution.

Prerequisite

Before you can use this new feature, the necessary config should be done below in SSCUI ID 103940 for those non-MAT receiver order.

Scenario

Let’s take order 1051263 as example, use app Order Cost Details - Event-Based

Click order details to check the result of order 1051263

Firstly ,click Related Apps and navigate to Display Posting Rules - Event-Based

And the posting rule is maintained as below to settle 100% to cost center KSII_1

Go back to order details screen, the amount of Transaction: Event-Based Settlement is 110 on credit side, which is equal of summary amount Transaction: Confirmations (100) and Transaction: Overhead (10) on debit side. And the settlement G/L account is 92105300, not the original accounts.

Furthermore, let’s take an order 2001061 settled before this new feature which can only choose settlement by original account.

Go to order details, the settlement uses original account.

Conclusion

This new feature provides more flexibility for specific requirement to use settlement G/L account.

entry.title: SSCUI: Maintain Route Determination维护路线确定
entry.content:
功能介绍

S/4HANA Cloud供应链中路线确定是一个重要流程, 用于自动为外向交货选择合适的运输路线。如果路线确定执行不正确, 会对业务产生以下影响:

  • 路线决定了关键的运输信息,如运输时间和方式。错误的路线可能导致交货日期不准确和不适当的运输方法。
  • 需要人工干预: 如果系统持续确定错误的路线, 可能需要频繁的手动更正,增加工作量和出错可能性。
  • 成本计算不准确: 由于路线影响运输成本, 错误的确定可能导致成本计算和定价不准确。

验证路线确定因素的配置：

  • 装运点数据(国家和出发区)
  • 销售凭证和客户主数据中的装运条件
  • 物料主数据中的运输组
  • 收货方数据(国家和运输区)
  • 路线是否在路线主数据中正确维护

当客户维护了错误路线确定记录时，客户需要删除或者更改这条记录，避免对客户带来糟糕的用户体验。

2502版本更新后，客户可以在SSCUI:Maintain Route Determination维护路线确定中删除不合适的记录。

感谢观看

entry.title: Mastering Test Data Container Usage in SAP Post-Upgrade Testing: A Comprehensive Guide
entry.content:
SAP Post-Upgrade Tests (PUT) is a crucial step in the SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud upgrade process, enabling system stability and business continuity. One of the most important factors influencing the efficiency of this phase is the effective use of Test Data Container (TDC). When leveraged correctly, TDCs can streamline automation efforts, reduce manual intervention, and enhance test accuracy and efficacy.

As an Expert in Test Automation Tool for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, I’m excited to share practical insights and best practices on optimizing TDC usage in PUT.

The Foundation: Identifying PUT Processes

The journey to efficient TDC usage begins with a comprehensive inventory of all PUT processes selected for execution. This initial step is crucial as it lays the groundwork for effective test data management within your TDC variant. By having a clear picture of all processes, you can ensure that no test scenario is overlooked and that your test data strategy is all-encompassing.

The Devil in the Details: Reviewing and Maintaining TDC Bindings

Once you have your process list, it's time to dive deep into each test process. The goal here is to identify and maintain all TDC bindings meticulously. This involves ensuring that all required and relevant field values are accurately maintained in the standard variant 'Variant_XX' of the respective standard TDCs. This step is critical in minimising data inconsistencies that could potentially derail your test execution.

Snapshot 1 showing relevant filed values being maintained in Variant_XX

The Safety Net: System Check on PUT Trigger

As you trigger PUT, the system performs an automatic validation - a crucial safety net in your testing process. This check ensures that all necessary TDC binding data is available in the standard TDC variant. If all bound fields have maintained data, the system proceeds with test execution using this data, bypassing the data picking logic. However, if any field is missing, the system falls back to the intelligent data picking logic, ensuring uninterrupted test execution.

The Time-Saver: Auto-Population of TDC Data

To further streamline the process, SAP offers an option for auto-population of bound test data containers. By providing consent, you allow the system to automatically* fill the standard TDC variant 'Variant_XX' with test data from the data picking logic  for standard post-upgrade test automates, if data isn't already maintained. This feature not only reduces manual effort but also ensures a more structured and automated test data management process.

Snapshot 2 showcasing provision of consent for auto-population of test data in Post-Upgrade Tests app

*Please review the automatically filled data in the variant for your automates

 The Maintenance Routine: Regular Data Review

As with any robust testing strategy, regular maintenance is key. For each release, it's crucial to review the data maintained in the standard variant and update it as necessary before triggering PUT. This proactive approach minimizes failures due to data inconsistencies and keeps your testing process smooth and efficient.

Conclusion:

Implementing these best practices for TDC usage in SAP Post-Upgrade Testing (PUT) can significantly enhance your testing efficiency. By leveraging system validations, automated data population, and structured test processes, you can achieve seamless execution while reducing manual effort and maintaining high data accuracy.

Remember, the key to successful PUT lies not just in automation, but in smart automation. By following these guidelines, you're not just running tests; you're optimizing your entire testing strategy, saving time, reducing errors, and ultimately, delivering a more robust SAP system to your organization.

As SAP systems continue to evolve, so too must our testing strategies. Stay tuned for more insights on SAP test automation, and don't hesitate to share your experiences with TDC usage in the comments below. Together, we can continue to refine and improve our SAP testing practices.

Useful Links :

  • Test Data Container Help Document
  • Post-Upgrade Tests Help Document

entry.title: SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 制造流程管理优化（三）审核订单进度-多级维度
entry.content:
欢迎来到我的博客。

     如我们所知，中国公有云客户群体大部分都采用了制造功能。在过去和客户的交流沟通中我们发现，产成品的层级越多，客户在跟踪产品的需求和交付上需要的精力越多，跨部门协同也越复杂。随着公有云产品创新开发路线图的发布，产品部门充分考虑到客户的痛点和需求，在2502制造交付新功能Review Order Progress来帮助客户克服管理难点和提升计划效率。

用途：
通过此应用，您可以查看所选销售订单、生产订单或流程订单的进度。该应用显示所有相关组件需求及其收货元素的多级视图，以及有关每个组件可能发生的短缺或其他问题的附加信息。

此应用包含在业务目录生产计划 - MRP 主控室 (SAP_SCM_BC_MRPCOCKPIT_MC) 中。

主要功能汇总：

  1. 显示组件需求和收货元素，以及订单结构多个级别上所选订单的所有相关需求和收货。支持的订单类型包括销售订单、流程订单或生产订单。
  2. 检查特定订单的生产或采购进度。检查是否有短缺或其他问题满足任何级别的任何组件的需求。
  3. 显示特定组件的延迟，称为组件延迟。还显示订单的总延迟。总延迟根据每个组件的建议需求日期计算。此日期考虑所选订单的需求日期以及不同的计划参数，例如，上级组件的计划交货时间或内部生产时间。
  4. 导航到其他应用以进一步处理或监控物料、需求元素或收货元素，例如，管理物料覆盖范围、显示物料库存、显示销售订单、显示计划订单、更改计划订单或显示采购申请。
  5. 将包含所有相关信息的组件结构导出到 Microsoft Excel 文件。

如何采用这个功能：

从抬头级别开始指定所有过滤器参数。标有红色*号的所有过滤器字段均为必填过滤器字段：

条目级别抬头级别 - 过滤器可能性

凭证选择
您可以指定、输入或选择想要显示的销售订单、生产订单或流程订单的订单类型和编号：

  • 对于销售订单，只需在输入字段中直接键入订单编号或使用值帮助。
  • 对于流程或生产订单，打开值帮助对话框并选择流程和生产订单搜索模板

 

然后，您可以搜索凭证并从结果清单中选择

搜索
首先，您可以输入准确的搜索词或通过输入部分搜索词使用通用搜索。

结果清单包括以下列的所有可能匹配项：

  • 材料
  • 工厂
  • MRP 控制员

仅问题
您可以使用过滤器 仅问题 仅显示已识别问题的组件的订单结构：

抬头级别 - 仅问题 是/否

 

选择 “是” 将显示的订单结构限制为已识别生产或采购问题的组件，包括其上级组件。

选择“否”显示包括所有组件的完整订单结构，无论它们是否有问题。

订单组件级别
过滤订单结构后，操作将在订单组件级别进行。我们来看看你可以获得的信息。

材料
该列显示所选订单的 物料清单 (BOM) 中包含的组件的物料编号和描述。如果组件多次包含在物料清单 (BOM) 中，例如，如果多个子组件需要该组件，则此列包括同一物料的多个条目。

订单组件级别 - 物料

 

物料组件显示在多级视图中，以指示所选订单中物料清单 (BOM) 的结构。显示每个组件的需求和收货信息，包括覆盖范围问题。

根据物料主数据中的定义，对物料使用特殊计划过程时，物料可能不会显示在订单树中。例如，考虑 MRP 类型 ND。此 MRP 类型代表“无计划”。在这种情况下，物料不会显示在订单树中。

从物料编号列中，您还可以打开每个组件的快速视图以访问附加信息，例如物料详细信息、MRP 数据、库存和短缺信息。

订单组件视图 - 物料的相关操作

问题
此列显示针对所选订单中包含的每个需求检测到的问题和警告的数量，及其严重程度。还指示每个组件的下级结构级别是否存在问题。

系统使用不同的颜色和图标检测以下问题并指示其关键性：

警告（非严重问题）显示为黄色：

  • 过去的未清日期 - 收货要素的未清日期是过去的日期。
  • 过去的开始日期 - 收货元素的基本开始日期是过去的日期。

错误（严重问题）以红色显示：

错误可能如下：

  • 过去的完成日期（收货元素的基本结束日期或交货日期是过去的日期）：

重新计划

  • 为避免物料短缺，应将收货重新计划到较早的日期。
  • 检查是否可以在管理物料覆盖范围应用中提前（重新计划）收货的可用日期。

主计划未提供的覆盖范围

  • 覆盖范围不足，因为计划收货根本未覆盖所需数量或仅部分覆盖所需数量。

您可以单击图标访问所选组件的所有检测到问题的清单，并按关键性（错误/警告）对其进行排序。要访问订单存在的所有问题的清单，请单击为结构的顶级节点显示的图标。此图标中显示的数字表示针对订单检测到的所有错误和警告的总和。

需求元素
此列显示每个物料组件存在的需求元素。

如果订单内的物料存在多个需求，则为每个需求元素显示单独的行。

比较需求和收货元素，有助于分析每个物料组件的需求和供应情况。表中可包含以下类型的需求元素：

  • CusOrd（销售订单）
  • DepReq（相关需求）
  • OrdDS（审批库存转储单）
  • OrdRes（相关预留）
  • 采购申请相关（审批库存转储申请）

您可以单击需求元素以打开包含更多详细信息的快速视图，或导航到其他应用以获取相关对象或进一步处理的信息。例如，您可以使用快速视图中的相关操作链接访问显示销售订单、显示计划订单或更改计划订单应用。

建议需求日期
显示每个组件所需数量的现有日期，以确保可以按时履行所选订单。

要确定此日期，系统会考虑所选销售订单、流程订单或生产订单的需求日期以及在物料主数据中定义的不同计划参数。

建议的需求日期可帮助您确定实际需要的特定组件早于还是晚于需求凭证中指定的日期。要进行计算，系统使用所选订单的需求日期并额外考虑不同的计划参数，具体取决于每个组件物料的采购类型：

  • 对于具有外部采购的上级组件的组件，系统从上级组件的建议需求日期中减去收货处理时间（工作日）和计划交货时间（以日历日计）。生成的日期是所选组件的建议需求日期。
  • 对于具有内部生产的上级组件的组件，系统从上级组件的建议需求日期中减去收货处理时间和内部生产时间（以工作日计）。生成的日期是所选组件的建议需求日期。

 

需求日期
需求日期来自凭证本身，例如来自销售订单的需求日期：

收货要素
此列显示物料组件的每个需求存在的收货要素。

比较需求和收货元素，有助于分析每个物料组件的需求和供应情况。表中可包含以下类型的收货元素：

  • PldOrd（计划订单）
  • 采购订单项目（采购订单项目/库存调拨单项目）
  • PrcOrd（流程订单）
  • 生产订单
  • 采购申请
  • 计划行
  • 库存（库存）

如果多个收货元素与需求相关，系统将显示最关键的 MRP 元素（最延迟的元素）和链接，您可以单击该链接以显示分配给需求的所有收货元素的概览。

请注意，此概览首先按可用性日期自动排序，然后按覆盖数量和收货元素排序。无法更改此排序。

单击特定 MRP 元素打开包含元素附加详细信息的快速视图，以及用于导航到其他应用以获取相关对象信息或进行进一步处理的选项。例如，您可以使用快速视图中的相关操作链接访问显示销售订单、显示计划订单或更改计划订单应用。

可用日期
此列显示收货元素数量的可用日期。

如果需求有多个收货元素，则最关键的收货元素（具有最大延迟的元素）的可用日期将显示在此列中。

如果可用性日期晚于组件的需求日期，系统会自动计算并在 组件延迟 列中显示延迟。

未覆盖数量
未覆盖数量是订单数量的一部分，既不能按时覆盖（覆盖数量），也不能延迟（延迟数量）。

未覆盖数量通过从缺少的数量中减去延迟数量来确定。

短缺数量
指定缺少的组件数量部分，以涵盖特定需求。

短缺数量包括延迟和未覆盖数量，通过从所需数量中减去覆盖数量来确定。

组件延迟
显示单个组件的收货数量可用性延迟的工作日天数。

对于组件的每个收货元素，系统会将需求日期与可用日期进行比较，以计算其延迟天数。如果可用日期晚于需求日期，则差异显示为组件的延迟，并以红色突出显示。

如果存在多个同一需求延迟的收货元素，则会在此列中显示最关键的延迟（最大值）。要单独显示每个收货元素的组件延迟，您可以单击收货元素列中的更多链接。

总延迟
显示在订单结构的特定级别，为所有下级组件的收货要素确定的最大延迟。

此列中显示的值可帮助您根据其基础组件的单独延迟，确定订单结构的组件实际延迟量。由于无法在所有下级物料组件可用之前满足所选订单的需求，因此订单结构顶级的总延迟始终与为结构较低级别确定的最大延迟相匹配。这同样适用于订单结构较低级别的组件需求，前提是这些需求已分配下级组件需求。

要在特定组件级别确定总延迟，系统会将建议需求日期与可用日期进行比较，并计算每个收货要素延迟的工作天数。如果需求存在多个收货元素，则会确定所有收货的最大延迟。第二步，系统检查订单结构较低级别的任何收货要素的总延迟是否较大，并在此列中显示最大延迟值。

延迟数量
延迟数量是稍后可以覆盖的订单数量的一部分，例如，因为首先需要由供应商提供，并且仅在需求日期之后接收。

从短缺数量中减去未覆盖数量来确定延迟数量。

覆盖数量
指定尚未发货给客户，但可以及时覆盖的订单数量部分，例如，基于可用库存或计划收货。

 

中文博文参考英文版本信息请参考New Review Order Progress - Multilevel View App - SAP Community

期待更多中国公有云客户采用此新功能，并反馈相关使用经验到PSCC GCR 产品咨询委员会。

谢谢。

entry.title: Preliminary What’s New Information for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2502.2
entry.content:
Preliminary versions of What’s New information and of the Product Assistance are now available for customers and partners to get a preview on the upcoming new and changed features of SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2502.2.

You can find this preview on the current Help Portal page SAP S/4HANA Cloud (see What’s New section “Preview of SAP S/4HANA Cloud 2502.2”). To access the Preliminary What’s New information, please use the What’s New Viewer or the Release Assessment and Scope Dependency (RASD) tool.

The RASD tool provides you with a personalized and tailored view on What’s New information based on the activated and used scope within your system. The preliminary information for SAP S/4HANA Cloud 2502.2 will be available in RASD within the next few days. We recommend using RASD to prepare for upgrades and updates of your system and to focus on the new and changed features that are relevant for you. A valid S-User ID is needed to use the RASD tool and to view all the preliminary content stated above.

For any feedback you may have, try the Commenting Feature for What's New documentation in this release. See this blog for more information.

entry.title: Discover the Updates with Event-Based Revenue Recognition in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2502.2
entry.content:
Introduction

SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition continues to evolve, bringing new features and enhancements that streamline business processes and improve financial reporting. Staying updated with these changes is crucial for businesses to leverage the full potential of their ERP system. In this blog, we will delve into two significant updates in the 2502.2 release within the Event-Based Revenue Recognition module of Revenue and Cost Accounting: the new item category for service orders and the changed custom source usage.

New Item Category for Service Orders

Overview: The latest release introduces a new item category, SVB7 Service Bundle (Ad Hoc Billing), designed to support ad hoc billing plans for service orders with Event-Based Revenue Recognition. This addition is a game-changer for businesses that require flexible billing options.

Business Details: SVB7 allows businesses to use Service Bundle in ad hoc billing plans, which are essential for managing service orders with event-based revenue recognition. This new item category is applicable for revenue recognition keys CCOCC and CCOFC, ensuring accurate and timely revenue recognition.

Technical Details: The introduction of SVB7 impacts several scope items, including 3M3 (Event-Based Revenue Recognition - Service Documents), 3M4 (Event-Based Revenue Recognition - Service Documents IFRS), and 3M5 (Event-Based Revenue Recognition - Service Documents - US GAAP). 

Expert Insight: Using SVB7 for service orders offers numerous benefits, such as enhanced flexibility in billing and improved accuracy in revenue recognition. This update can significantly streamline business processes and ensure compliance with financial reporting standards.

Changed Custom Source Usage in Event-Based Revenue Recognition

Overview: The 2502.2 release also brings changes to custom source usage in event-based revenue recognition. The configuration activity Maintain Settings for Event-Based Revenue Recognition has been simplified, narrowing down to a single account assignment rule, COSPTM. It's used for time and material scenarios. To learn more about custom sources, see 3537769.

Business Details: Use custom sources if you want to split recognized revenue by posting to different accounts. Previously, assignment rule usages 400 and 401 were required, but with this update, they are no longer necessary. This change is particularly relevant for the configuration activity Maintain Settings for Event-Based Revenue Recognition. 

Technical Details: The affected scope items include 1IL (Event-Based Revenue Recognition - Project-Based Services), 1P0 (Event-Based Revenue Recognition - Project-Based Services IFRS), 33O (Event-Based Revenue Recognition - Project-Based Services US GAAP), and several others. 

Expert Insight: Simplifying custom source usage by narrowing down to COSPTM offers several advantages. It reduces complexity in configuration and implementation processes, making it easier for businesses to maintain accurate revenue recognition settings.

Conclusion

The latest updates in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2502.2 bring valuable enhancements that can significantly improve business operations. The new item category for service orders and the changed custom source usage in event-based revenue recognition offer greater flexibility and simplicity. Businesses should explore these features to leverage their benefits fully and stay ahead in the competitive landscape.

entry.title: What’s New in SAP Build – Q1 2025 Release Highlights
entry.content:
SAP Build continues to evolve to support how modern teams work — now with more artificial intelligence, flexibility, and speed. In Q1 2025, we delivered a wave of innovations across SAP Build Code, Process Automation, Apps, and Work Zone to help developers and business users turn ideas into enterprise-ready solutions faster than ever.

Whether you're building custom apps, creating SAP S/4HANA extensions, or streamlining processes, these updates will help you move from concept to value more efficiently.  Let's review the top 10 updates we selected from last quarter.

Licensing and Availability Updates

1. SAP Task Center Now included with SAP Build Work Zone

SAP Task Center is now embedded in all SAP Build Work Zone editions, giving users a unified view of workflow tasks from multiple SAP systems — right within their Work Zone homepage. No separate integration is required. This update also extends to partner licensing, making including Task Center in custom Work Zone scenarios easier. The result is faster approvals, centralized task tracking, and higher productivity for business users and managers — all in one workspace.

2. SAP Build Apps Now Available in More Regions

You can now deploy SAP Build Apps in EU11 and Sydney data centers, bringing your applications to more users. The increased coverage improves app responsiveness, supports region-specific compliance needs, and enables global scalability for your enterprise use cases.

Joule for Developers Updates

3. Generate End-to-End Workflows with a Prompt

In SAP Build Process Automation, developers can now describe business scenarios in natural language — and let generative AI create forms, decisions, and process flows automatically. Powered by SAP's purpose-built large language models (LLMs), this capability delivers contextualized, SAP-aware outcomes that speed up workflow creation and reduce manual effort. It also empowers business users to contribute to process design without writing code.

4. Generate UI Pages from Data in SAP Build Apps

SAP Build Apps now uses generative AI to propose user interface pages based on your data model. For example, if your app contains entities like Books, Authors, or Genres, Joule recommends corresponding screens, layouts, and controls. This helps teams move quickly from data modeling to interactive UI design, shortening prototyping cycles and improving design consistency.

5. Use Generative AI for Fiori UI Creation in SAP Build Code

Developers can now use Joule in SAP Build Code to generate Fiori-based UIs for existing CAP apps by entering the command /add-ui or selecting the Joule icon in the Storyboard. Joule understands your application structure and proposes relevant layouts, controls, and navigation patterns, helping you scaffold front-end apps quickly and focus on customization instead of starting from scratch.

6. Multi-Agent Support for CAP App Generation

SAP Build Code now uses multi-agent support powered by Joule to generate complete CAP applications. Each agent focuses on a specific part — like data models, services, or UI — so you get a more complete project from a single prompt. This enhancement saves time and helps you focus on business logic instead of building everything from scratch.

Read also Overview of all GenAI Options in SAP Build Code by david_bizer.

Enhance SAP Business Suite Applications

7. Extend SAP S/4HANA with Custom UIs (Planned for Release Soon)

Coming very soon in SAP S/4HANA Cloud, Public Edition, developers will be able to use the SAP Build Extensibility Wizard to create side-by-side extensions—either by customizing existing SAP Fiori apps or building entirely new web applications. This approach enables teams to meet specific business needs while preserving the core system, supporting clean core principles and long-term agility.

  • See documentation: Using the Extensibility Wizard.
  • On setup, read the blog SAP Build Extensibility Wizard for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition by @Karthik-Thiru. 

8. New Enhancements for Guided Experiences by SAP Build

New enhancements to Guided Experiences by SAP Build help simplify complex processes like employee onboarding, parental leave, procurement approvals, and IT service management, where multiple steps, systems, and stakeholders are involved. With this release, ready-to-use content packages for SAP SuccessFactors Employee Onboarding and Cross-boarding are now available in the SAP Build Store—enabling faster rollout of consistent, step-by-step guidance for new hires and internal transfers.

  • To get started: Guided Experiences powered by SAP Build by florian_buech.
  • Watch the 2-minute video: Navigating Parental Leave with SAP Build Guided Experiences.

9. Intermediate Message Events for Real-Time Workflows

With new support for intermediate message events, SAP Build Process Automation lets workflows pause until specific API-triggered inputs are received and resume automatically. This powerful capability reduces wait times and manual monitoring, helping teams design more adaptive and responsive processes with fewer errors and overhead. 

Boost Developer Productivity

10. Revamped Project Overview in SAP Build Code

A redesigned Project Overview in SAP Build Code provides a clean, tree-based view of all app components, supporting CAP Java and CAP Node. This enhanced layout integrates with the Storyboard, making it easier for developers to navigate complex projects, onboard new team members, and stay focused while building.

Ready to See These Updates in Action?

These are just a few of the top updates rolling out this quarter. Want to explore the new features in more detail, see live demos, and get your questions answered by product experts?

👉Join us on April 15 for the What's New in SAP Build Q1 2025 webinar.
Reserve your spot to discover these updates in action. Register below:

📅There are two options to cover different time zones.
🕘9:00 – 10:00 AM CET → https://sap.to/604200bfg
🕔5:00 – 6:00 PM CET → https://sap.to/604500bfc

Learn More

For a complete list of new features, check out the following resources:

  • SAP Build Help Portal
  • SAP Build Road Map Explorer

Please stay current and connect with fellow SAP Builders by joining our Application Development and Automation Community for the latest updates, activities, and challenges.

entry.title: What’s New in SAP Central Business Configuration 2502 FD1
entry.content:
With the latest feature delivery (FD1) for SAP Central Business Configuration, exciting new features are available. This blog post will provide you with a comprehensive overview of these features.

Enhanced Flexibility with the Parallel Line

SAP Central Business Configuration now offers a powerful feature for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, 3-system landscape, called the parallel line. This innovation enables you to make business configuration enhancements more flexibly and safely without disrupting your daily operations. With this feature, SAP Central Business Configuration will feed configuration settings into two lines. Here’s how it works:

The main line is where your initial implementation and daily operations take place. You can define your scope, set up your organizational structure, configure and test your business processes, and finally use them productively.

The parallel line is a separate, safe environment that’s used to make and test business configuration enhancements, without impacting the daily operations in the main line. To this end, your workspace is copied, including all your business configuration. Once you’ve made and tested all enhancements, you can merge them back into the main line. This is particularly useful for the rollout of new countries/regions or other large-scale business configuration enhancements.

Dropdown menu to switch between the main line and the parallel line

Key benefits of the parallel line include more flexibility in project handling, better risk mitigation, and seamless lifecycle management. By leveraging this innovation, you can optimize your implementation and ongoing operations for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition.

Optimized Performance for Organizational Structure Management

When you open the Set Up Organizational Structure app, you’ll notice that the units of your organizational structure are partially collapsed, and similar units are stacked to group them together. This enhances system performance and improves the user experience, particularly beneficial for large and complex organizational structures.

Partially collapsed and stacked units in the Set Up Organizational Structure app

Simplified Address Maintenance

In the Set Up Organizational Structure app, we've furthermore simplified the address maintenance by reducing the amount of data you need to enter. Now, you only need to fill in the country or region and city. Detailed addresses are managed in SAP S/4HANA Cloud.

Simplified side panel in the Set Up Organizational Structure app

Streamlined Scope Definition: Removal of Sector Selection

To simplify the user experience, we’ve removed the sector selection in the Define Scope app. Instead of preselecting the sector, you now choose directly the bundle applicable to your business.

For the public sector, you can directly add the Public Sector Management bundle to your scope. This is particularly relevant for government, non-profit, and educational organizations that need to comply with funds and budget accounting.

For the private sector, you can directly add any of the other bundles to your scope as suitable. This makes the process more straightforward and intuitive.

Bundles for the private sector and the public sector in the Define Scope app

For a quick summary, check out this video about What's New in 2502 FD1. Don’t forget to also use the What’s New Viewer on the SAP Help Portal, which provides further details and links about all the recent changes.

That wraps up the new features this time. Did you like this blog? If so, please leave us a thumbs up at the top. Also, make sure to follow the SAP Central Business Configuration tag or follow this dedicated blog collection to stay up to date with the latest blog posts.

Do you have any ideas on how to further enhance SAP Central Business Configuration? Go to the SAP Customer Influence platform to submit an improvement request or vote for the ideas of others. This helps us understand your requirements even better and prioritize our work accordingly. We’re looking forward to your improvement requests.

entry.title: SAP Enterprise Support Academy Newsletter April 2025
entry.content:
Dear customers,
Find here an overview of the SAP Enterprise Support Academy monthly newsletter on latest news, training courses, upcoming sessions, and further educational offerings.
The SAP Enterprise Support Academy provides expert guidance and high impact knowledge transfer services that enable you to innovate and be successful with your SAP solutions.

Subscribe to regularly receive latest news and stay informed about the wide range of educational offerings provided by the SAP Enterprise Support Academy that help you to build the digital skills tailored to your needs.

Preview available live sessions on SAP Support Portal, tailored per region:
APJ | EMEA | Latin America | North America*

 

SAP Cloud ALM for Service: Find Updates Regularly in Your Service Results Report

Learn how to optimize your Service Results Report and get the most from SAP reporting. Stay updated on technical and administrative changes to SAP Cloud ALM for Service: Service Results Report using the SAP Companion Help Tool.

Check out the step-by-step guide

Need to Integrate SAP LeanIX with SAP Signavio or ServiceNow?

New E-Learning sessions simplify SAP LeanIX integrations with ServiceNow and SAP Signavio. Find details about these courses and how to get personalized insights for your SAP Signavio solution through our Business Transformation Management Value Map: 

Learn more

New E-Learning about SAP BusinessObjects BI - Hybrid Integration

Watch out our republished blog for a new e-learning showing how you can connect SAP Web Intelligence to your SAP Datasphere analytic models using OAuth 2.0 a great way to maximize your existing SAP BusinessObjects investment while also leveraging your cloud data and taking on some SAP Datasphere use cases!

SAP BusinessObjects BI - Hybrid Integration

Maximize Your SAP Integrated Business Planning Experience with Continuous Quality Checks (CQC)

One of the key aspects of a successful implementation is ensuring the quality and stability of your solution. Learn about the available CQC services that help you support this goal and how to get started!

Continuous Quality Checks for SAP IBP Implementation

All you Need to Know About Continuous Quality Checks for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

Join our new E-learning to learn how you can benefit from Continuous Quality Checks (CQCs), which are remote services that help reduce implementation risks, analyze your system and provide tailored improvement recommendations. Get an overview of the available CQCs for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition.

Full details in the blog post

Supporting Your SAP LeanIX Journey: Learning Assets to Get Started and Manage Integrations

To better support your business transformation journey, we’ve expanded our content to include learning assets for SAP LeanIX in the Business Transformation Value Map. This addition from SAP Enterprise Support provides enhanced resources to help manage IT landscapes and drive digital transformation. Sign up for Value Maps today to access this content and receive personalized guidance for your business transformation journey.

Full blog

Which SAP Sales Cloud Capabilities Make Sense for Your Business?

To help businesses identify the right capabilities, we’ve created an e-learning course called "Increase Sales Force Efficiency." This is a great starting point to explore the features of SAP Sales Cloud and determine which ones suit your business. We’ve also developed a more detailed course, 'Value Package to Increase Your Sales Force Efficiency,' which offers deeper insights, guidance, and additional resources. Read our blog post for details and join the Value Map for SAP Customer Experience Solutions for expert guidance–at no additional cost.

Full details

Prepare Your SAP Fiori Implementation for SAP S/4HANA

Are you responsible for defining a UX adoption strategy for your business and looking to gain hands-on experience with the activities, technologies, and tools needed to implement SAP Fiori UX? Learn more about the program and join Value Maps for expert guidance tailored to your unique SAP Fiori and SAP S/4HANA journey.

Check it out here (value maps access required)

This innovative feature finds the most relevant answers to your query from across the SAP knowledge ecosystem—from help documentation to Knowledge Base Articles—and compiles them into a single, concise solution at the top of your knowledge search results.

To learn more, check out this blog post 

Gain the Skills Needed for Your Journey with SAP S/4HANA

Make yourself ready to take the next step on your SAP S/4HANA conversion by joining on of our five Live Must-Know sessions. From Fundamentals to Readiness Check and Sandbox Conversion to Finance and Custom Code Adaptation, you find the right course for your needs. They are interactive and full of “must-know” facts, often feature demos and you are encouraged to ask questions throughout the session.

Pick your topic here

SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition: Available Meet-the-Expert Sessions and E-Learnings

Are you an SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition customer and interested in learning more about the products and tools available to help you run effectively? If so, then find here a great list of courses available to you. One of them is the upcoming Meet-the-Expert session on April 16 about Situation Handling in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public/Private Edition.

Check out the full blog and pick the topic most relevant to you

Join our Biweekly Open Office Hours: SAP Cloud Identity Services (IAS/IPS)

SAP Identity Authentication Services (IAS) is the go-forward approach across many SAP solutions, and a prerequisite for Joule. We host regular Office Hours for SAP customers and partners to get SAP expert support and/or advice.
Register for our regular Office Hours for the following SAP solutions:

SAP SuccessFactors HCM sessions register here
Ariba and Fieldglass sessions register here

SAP's AI Solutions for Customer Loyalty and Efficiency

SAP’s support Artificial Intelligence scenarios are creating seamless, efficient experiences for both customers and employees. AI engines in SAP for Me are providing self-service opportunities and helping create well-documented cases along the way, while Generative AI and Robotic Process Automation (RPA) are automating repetitive tasks. This fosters effortless experiences and stronger customer loyalty.

Watch the video (YouTube)

Black Friday & Beyond: Revolutionizing Support with AI-Powered Prevention

In this episode of SAP Change Makers, Stefan Steinle, EVP, and Head of Customer Support and Cloud Lifecycle Management, delves into the transformative role of Customer Support in driving business success. Highlighting SAP's groundbreaking Black Friday Cyber Monday (BFCM) Readiness Program,he shares how proactive strategies and innovative technologies like AI, self-service solutions, and Mission-Critical Support help customers thrive during peak demand. Learn how SAP safeguards operations, prevents issues, and continuously optimizes for cloud excellence, ensuring seamless experiences for businesses worldwide.

Tune in for a visionary look at the future of customer support and its role in enabling intelligent enterprises.

Join our hands-on remote workshop starting April 28, 2025 to explore Generative AI in SAP SuccessFactors HCM. This Expert-guided Implementation (EGI) offers hands-on experience and expert support. Learn AI features, validate requirements, set up use cases, and more. Sign up now and enhance your skills with SAP’s latest innovations – included with your SAP Cloud subscription!

entry.title: Upcoming Removal of SAP Fiori Themes Belize and Blue Crystal
entry.content:
The SAP Fiori visual themes Belize and Blue Crystal have been deprecated for many years. Now the time has come to optimize our UI technology by removing these themes. We plan to remove them in the next releases of products which allow users to choose multiple themes, such as SAP S/4HANA Cloud and SAP S/4HANA (on-premise) and SAP Integrated Business Planning.

Over the years SAP Fiori has evolved, introducing new visual themes along the way – most recently the Horizon theme. The Horizon theme not only looks modern and appealing, it also provides the highest level of support for accessibility, as well as the most flexibility for customers to create their own custom themes.

Horizon is the visual theme used by all our products in the SAP Business Suite. Figures 1 – 3 below show some examples of Horizon in our products.

We strongly recommend that all users use the Horizon theme to ensure an optimal and consistent user experience.

 

Removal of Belize and Blue Crystal

The visual themes Belize and Blue Crystal will be removed from SAP products with their next releases, starting in the second half of 2025. This means that the upcoming releases of SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition and SAP Integrated Business Planning, as well as further products which currently include one or both of these themes, will no longer provide them as options.

After upgrading, existing usages of Belize or Blue Crystal will be automatically redirected to the Horizon theme.

If you have created your own custom theme based on Belize or Blue Crystal using the UI theme designer, you will need to migrate this to an available theme. Our strong recommendation is to base the new version on the Horizon theme.

For more information about the UI technologies affected, and how to migrate custom themes, have a look at this blog post by Oliver Graeff:

  • Announcement: Removal of Belize theme of SAP Fiori.

Additional Information for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

Important: If you use a custom theme and have set up the UI Theme Designer connection to your SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition with a communication arrangement on SAP Business Technology Platform Neo, make sure to move it to Cloud Foundry before you upgrade. Otherwise the automatic redirect to Horizon will not work. Also, future innovations will require the communication arrangement to use Cloud Foundry, so doing this move makes sense anyway. Here is a link to the documentation for this:

  • Integrating UI Theme Designer – SAP BTP Cloud Foundry Environment.

 

Examples of SAP Fiori visual theme Horizon

You can see some examples for Horizon in figures 1 – 3: for SAP Start and SAP Mobile Start, for SAP S/4HANA Cloud (the images show SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2502) and SAP SuccessFactors. For further examples, have a look at these blog posts:

  • SAP Fiori Update: More Products with Horizon, Entry Point strategy, UX news for SAP S/4HANA and More.
  • SAP User Experience Update: Yet More Products with the New Look driven by SAP Fiori.
  • Improvements Coming to the Horizon Visual Theme for SAP Fiori.
  • SAP UX Innovations in Public Cloud Products, Joule, AI, Entry Points, Apple Vision Pro and more.
  • SAP UX Latest 2024 Innovations in Public Cloud: Joule, GenAI and More.
  • SAP User Experience Q1/2025 Update – Part 1: Many New Innovations Available (AI, Joule and More) – along with parts 2 – 7 which are linked therein.

Figure 1: The SAP Fiori visual theme Horizon for SAP Start and SAP Mobile Start. ALT Text: the image shows a screenshot of SAP Start with the Horizon theme, three iPhones with Horizon (one dark and one light showing SAP Mobile start, one showing an iPhone start screen with a notification from SAP Mobile STart ), and an Apple Watch with a notification from SAP Mobile Start and Horizon.

Figure 2: The SAP Fiori visual theme Horizon for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition. ALT Text: an image showing two sceens with Morning Horizon (the My Home page and Monitor Purchase Order Items analytical list page) and two screens with the same apps with Evening Horizon, plus the text “Also available for accessibility: high contrast light and dark themes”.

Figure 3: Examples of SAP SuccessFactors applications with the SAP Fiori visual theme Horizon. ALT Text: Three examples showing both web and mobile: on the left the Home Page, in the middle Team Goals, on the right Opportunity Marketplace showing many cards, each displaying an opportunity.

To find out more about the Horizon theme, have a look at the SAP Fiori for web design guidelines:

  • Theming.

entry.title: Event Based Revenue Recognition - Provider Contract (Subscription Billing) - (5DQ)
entry.content:
The integration of Subscription Billing with event-based revenue recognition allows you to recognize revenue for subscriptions with customers that are processed as provider contracts.

It is possible to conduct revenue recognition for subscriptions created in SAP Subscription Billing. These subscriptions are stored as provider contract items in SAP S/4 HANA Cloud. With event-based revenue recognition, you can therefore manage revenue recognition for provider contracts. Revenue for provider contract items is recognised based on the billing plan associated with the provider contract item.

 

Overview of the End-to-End Process

Integration between Subscription Billing and S4HANA Cloud

For interactive overview, please visit SAP Help page.

Revenue Recognition Methods for Provider Contracts

Revenue Recognition Key

Revenue Recognition Methods for Provider Contracts

PPC

Provider Contracts 360 Days Calendar Year - No costs deferred

Time-based revenue recognition which is based on billing plan with a 360 days calendar year. No costs are deferred.

PPCA

Provider Contracts 365 Days Calendar Year - No costs deferred

Time-based revenue recognition which is based on billing plan with a 365 days calendar year. No costs are deferred.

PPCN

Periodic Provider Contracts - No revenue recognition

Recognise revenue as accrued

 

In this blog, we will focus on the Revenue Recognition PPCA (365 days) key with also the freeze period in between.

So, we have subscription billing for 1 year. However, there is a freeze period for 6 months after contract started for 3 months. During the freeze period (April - September), there should not be any revenue recognition.

Let's review the subscription billing, 

Contract start date: 01.01.2024

Contract end date: 31.12.2024 

In the line item 2, we have recurring charge type (02) and we maintained Rev Rec Key PPCA.

However, we also have a freeze period between April - September. The contract resume on 1st October, 2024.

This is the result of Event-Based Revenue Recognition - Provider Contracts.

We can use Product and Margin Fiori App to see the Revenue, Cost and Margin in Provider Contract. And as expected, we do not have any revenue from period 3 to period 9.

Quick look on the SSCUI 105138 - Derivation of Recognition Key for Provider Contracts, we can define the Rev Rec Key based on Company code, Charge Type and Product dimension.

I can recommend to read this blog Subscription Billing with Convergent Invoicing and Contract-Based Revenue Recognition from @volkmar_zahn

entry.title: S/4 Hana Public Cloud - Workforce Management Perspective
entry.content:
To Begin , this article is for people who are planning to implement S/4HANA Public cloud along with Employee Central as the frontend for HR data.

SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition is a ready-to-run enterprise resource planning (ERP) system that delivers the latest industry best practices and business application solutions on a public cloud network. It offers an efficient, agile, and affordable solution for connecting your workforce, suppliers, customers, markets, and assets without adding complexity. 

 

HR in S/4 HANA Public cloud

 

For HR processes, SAP recommends using SAP SuccessFactors solutions, which are seamlessly integrated with SAP S/4HANA Cloud. In S/4 HANA cloud employees are termed as workforce persons and process of managing their data is referred as workforce management.

 

HR information is processed and stored in HR system. However, basic employee information is required to execute various processes in S/4 system such as project planning, billing, project time recording etc. This information is made available through workforce integration

 

SAP SuccessFactors system can be integrated with S/4 system through Master Data Integration covered in scope ID JB1 Core HR with SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central (JB1) . This scope supports transfer of employee data (including contingent workers). It includes basic data, such as employee name and ID, and contact details, such as workplace address, e-mail, and telephone information.

 

Below are relevant scope ids where integrated HR data is used

J12- Time Recording - Project-Based Services (J12)

6JO- Resource Management for Projects (6JO)

Integration (MDI)

SAP Master Data Integration service is a central master data hub. Applications integrate with SAP Master Data Integration to synchronize their local master data databases with the master data database of the central hub.

Data flow and field mapping

SAP Master Data Integration enables you to share consistent master data across multiple products easily and efficiently. Once the relevant products, applications, or landscapes are connected to SAP Master Data Integration, master data is synchronized between them.

Following order for data flow is recommended for successful data replication between S/4 HANA Public cloud and SAP SuccessFactors.

Step

Entity

Source

Destination

1

Company Code

S/4 HANA Public Cloud

SAP SuccessFactors

2

Cost Center

S/4 HANA Public Cloud

SAP SuccessFactors

3

Bank Data

S/4 HANA Public Cloud

SAP SuccessFactors

4

Workforce person

SAP SuccessFactors

S/4 HANA Public Cloud

5

Availability information

SAP SuccessFactors

S/4 HANA Public Cloud

Replication of master data such as Company code, cost center and bank data to SuccessFactors determines base to process employee information and maintain data which will be uniform once it is replicated back to S/4 HANA Public cloud.

Field Mapping between S/4 HANA Public Cloud and SuccessFactors

SAP has provided pre-defined field mappings for most of the entities when Employee master data is replicated from SuccessFactors to S/4 HANA. SAP note 3078790 provides a list of mapping and configuration activities required in case of custom data handling.

Integration Process

 

We will cover end to end integration process from both S/4 and SF systems. The process of configuring integration should follow below sequence. These steps are described in detail.

 

 

S.No.

Step

In which system to perform the step

1.

Creating a service instance and a service key

SAP Business Technology Platform

2.

Configure a business system ID

SAP Business Technology Platform

3.

Configure write permissions

SAP Business Technology Platform

4.

Create user in SF for communication

SAP SuccessFactors

5.

Creating communication systems and communication arrangements

SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

6.

Configuring Destinations

SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

7.

Configure distribution models in Business Data Orchestration

SAP Business Technology Platform

8.

Configure OAuth connection

SAP SuccessFactors

9.

Configuration for Master Data Integration for all object types

SAP SuccessFactors

10.

Enable Address Mapping

SAP SuccessFactors

11.

Configure Business Scenario

SAP SuccessFactors

12.

Configure Availability Iflow

SAP Integration Suite

 

 

  1. Creating a service instance and a service key

For each application you connect, you will have to create a dedicated service instance of SAP Master Data Integration from the SAP BTP marketplace. After creating the service instance, you create a service binding to obtain the credentials to establish the technical connectivity to the application.

 

  • Navigate to Services > Service Marketplace and select Master Data Integration. Choose Create and enter information required.
  • Service - select Master Data Integration
  • Service plan- select sap-integration. This service plan must be chosen for connecting SAP-branded cloud applications (e.g., SAP S/4HANA Cloud).
  • Provide instance name
  • In the next screen configure service instance parameters. Provide a value for the mandatory application attribute based on the application that you intend to connect. We have maintained “sfsf” as provided by SAP for SAP SuccessFactors Application (ref. Connecting Applications via Service Instances | SAP Help Portal )

2.Configure a business system ID and write permissions

The businessSystemId acts as a display name for the client represented by the service instance in the Business Data Orchestration UI. Also, the businessSystemId configuration attribute is mandatory to connect a client using the SOAP API for Business Partner.

 

You should only configure businessSystemId if the documentation of the connected application demands it.

 

  • Navigate to the service instance.
  • Select ... -> View Parameters to view the current attribute configuration of the service instance.
  • Copy the JSON object with the current state of attributes.
  • Select ... -> Update. Paste the existing JSON object and add, update, or remove the relevant attributes. Note that the update operation does not patch the existing configuration, it replaces the full state of configuration. Therefore, it is important to provide all relevant attributes in the update operation.
  • Add the following attribute to configure the businessSystemId.

 

"businessSystemId": "<businessSystemId>"

 

  • The businessSystemId attribute should have a length of up to 60 characters and it must be unique within a tenant.
  • Add the following attribute to configure the writePermissions

 

"writePermissions": [

                                                            { "entityType": "<entityTypeOdmName1>" },

                                                            { "entityType": "<entityTypeOdmName2>" },

                                                            ...

                                                            ]

Example-

"writePermissions": [

                                             { "entityType": "sap.odm.finance.costobject.CostCenter" },

                                             ]

 

 

3.Creating communication systems

  • Navigate to Communication Systems app. Create New system
  • Define ID and name
  • Enter General and contact details
  • Host Name- Enter the host name of the remote system for outgoing API service calls. Please note that the usage of localhost is not allowed as host name.
  • Enter users for inbound and outbound communication.

 

 

4.Create communication arrangements

Create below communication arrangements to allow data replication of our required objects:

  • SAP_COM_0001 Employee Integration
  • SAP_COM_0644 Photo Replication
  • SAP_COM_0056 Employee Central Cost Center Integration
  • SAP_COM_0379 Bank - Bank Integration
  • SAP_COM_0441 Employee Central Financial Master Data Integration
  • SAP_COM_0914 Workforce Availability Integration
  • SAP_COM_0301External HR Workforce Integration                                        
  • SAP_COM_0594Business Data Orchestration Integration                             
  • SAP_COM_0659SAP Master Data Integration                                                   
  • SAP_COM_0943Business Object Cost Center Integration

5.Configuring Destinations

These steps are required to set up a destination for the connection to Business Data Orchestration

  • Go to your SAP BTP Cockpit under Subaccount  Connectivity  Destinations.
  • Provide below details to create new destination:
  • Name
  • Type- HTTP
  • URL- Provide the service URL from the MDO Distribution Administration OData V4 service. Look for MDO: Distribution Administration among the inbound services with application protocol OData V4 that was generated while creating the communication arrangement SAP_COM_0594, and copy the entire URL into this field.
  • Create different distribution model for each object type to be replicated with URL specified for each object type.
  • User and Password: As defined for the user assigned to the communication system

 

Workforce data

URL- https://my<number>-api.s4hana.cloud.sap/sap/opu/odata4/sap/mdo_distributionadmin/srvd_a2x/sap/distributionadmin/0001/

 

Bank Data

URL- https://my<number>-api.s4hana.cloud.sap/sap/opu/odata4/sap/api_bank/srvd_a2x/sap/bank/0003/Bank

 

Cost center

URL- https://my<number>-api.s4hana.cloud.sap/sap/opu/odata4/sap/api_cost_center/srvd_a2x/sap/costcenter/0001/A_CostCenterText_2

 

 

 

 

6.Configure distribution models in Business Data Orchestration

Create separate distribution models for each object type with details as below:

  • Model    name- Customer specific name for each model
  • Business Object Type- Respective business object type for each object type
    • Workforce Person- sap.odm.workforce.WorkforcePerson
    • Bank Data- sap.odm.finance.bank.Bank
    • Cost center- sap.odm.finance.costobject.CostCenter
  • Mode- Mode of data flow
    • Workforce Person- Pull
    • Bank Data- Push
    • Cost Center- Create 2 distribution models one with Push mode and one with Pull mode
  • Provider interface- choose respective API with latest version
    • Workforce Person- MDI_LOG_WORKFORCE_PERSON
    • Bank Data- MDI_LOG_BANK
    • Cost Center- MDI_LOG_COST_CENTER(Pull)/ MDI_CHANGE_COST_CENTER(Push)
  • Consumer- Choose a consumer destination for the distribution model created in previous section.

 

 

 

7.Configure OAuth connection

  •           Go to Admin Center > Security Center > OAuth Configurations
  •              Add new configuration
  •              Grant Type: Client_Credentials
  •              Client ID: Value from clientid in the service key credentials
  •              Client Secret: Value from clientsecret in the service key credentials
  •              Token URL: Value from url in the service key credentials + /oauth/token

 

8.Configuration for Master Data Integration for all object types

  • Go to Manage Data> Create new > SAP Master Data Integration Configuration
  • Select Integration object as Bank
  • Select Parameter as “Is Migration object”
  • Provide response value as No. Save the configuration.
  • Repeat new configuration for integration object as Workforce Person.
  • Select and add parameter for photo replication if photo replication is required.
  • Provide cutoff dates by selecting relevant parameters.

 

9.Enable Address Mapping

  • Go to SAP SuccessFactors Upgrade Center and search for Best Practices Address Mapping for SAP Master Data Integration.
  • Select the option and choose Upgrade Now. Address mapping is configured automatically.

 

 

10.Configure Business Scenarios

  • You would need to run/schedule certain business scenarios from EC in order to trigger master data replication. These can be found under Admin Center> Business Scenarios
  • Bank/ Cost Center Replication from SAP Master Data Integration to EC – You need to provide Oauth config , API URL in source settings
  • Provide operation URI specific for object type-
  • Bank- /v0/odm/3.0.0/log/sap.odm.finance.bank.Bank
  • Cost center- /v0/odm/3.1.0/log/sap.odm.finance.costobject.CostCenter

 

  • For Workforce Replication- Provide details in destination settings. Set HTTP Method as POST
  •  

  •  

  •  

 

 

 

 

11.Configure Availability Iflow

 

  •    Select integration package and artifact

Select integration package as “SAP SuccessFactors - Employee Availability Integration with SAP S/4HANA Cloud” and artifact as “SFSF_EC_to_S4HANA_Availability”

  •    Configuration

Go to Security material under Manage security section and maintain the user and passwords for S/4 HANA and SuccessFactors systems.

 

In the artifact below screen appears. Click on configuration and set configuration values for the integration.

 

  • Set schedule of replication as required

 

 

 

Business Partner and Vendor creation

 

A work assignment defines the relationship between a worker and a specific company or legal entity, also known as a work agreement. Each work assignment is uniquely identified by a Work Assignment ID.

 

A workforce person can have multiple work assignments, either at different times or simultaneously. When there are multiple assignments, the system automatically determines the main employment, which indicates the current active employment.

 

Each workforce person and their work assignments are represented by various business partners, each with a specific role.

 

Points to note-

  • To represent an internal workforce person or employee, one BP with the Employee role is created.
  • For each work assignment for an employee, a separate BP with the Employment role is created.
  • Bank data is stored on the employment BP. If bank data is maintained in employment BP, the Supplier (FLVN00) role is added to employment BP automatically.

 

Conclusion :- This Article gives you Indepth insight of the workfoce managment and integration between successfactors to S4/Hana Public cloud. 

In the next Blog , the aspects of integration with Resource management will be covered including talent intelligence hub and availability control from EC to Resource management.

Thanks to Divya Gopal , Amruta Chitale , Arghya Sengupta , Mahesh Joshi  , Nitin Patil , Sourabh Panigrahi , Mayank Barui from My current Organization to help and contribute to this Blog.
